part.text: I’ll inspect the target-side `MaxPooling3dGradGradOp` context and validate the minimal equivalent hunk before editing the checkout.

The target implementation is the same op, but it lacks the newer branch’s pre-existing empty-tensor checks. I’m keeping the backport limited to the security fix itself: stop after invalid `Pool3dParameters`, then enforce matching element counts before launching the kernel.

part.text: The hunk applies cleanly against the target base. I’m applying that validated change to the current checkout.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: tensorflow/core/kernels/pooling_ops_3d.cc
@@
     Pool3dParameters params{context,  ksize_,       stride_,
                             padding_, data_format_, tensor_in.shape()};
+    if (!context->status().ok()) return;  // params is invalid
 
     Tensor* output = nullptr;
     OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->forward_input_or_allocate_output(
                                 {2}, 0, tensor_out.shape(), &output));
 
+    OP_REQUIRES(context,
+                tensor_in.NumElements() == out_grad_backprop.NumElements(),
+                errors::InvalidArgument("tensor_in and out_grad_backprop must "
+                                        "have same number of elements, got <",
+                                        tensor_in.DebugString(), "> and <",
+                                        out_grad_backprop.DebugString(), ">"));
+    OP_REQUIRES(
+        context, tensor_out.NumElements() == output->NumElements(),
+        errors::InvalidArgument(
+            "tensor_out and output must have same number of elements, got <",
+            tensor_out.DebugString(), "> and <", output->DebugString(), ">"));
+
     LaunchMaxPooling3dGradGradOp<Device, T>::launch(
         context, params, tensor_in, tensor_out, out_grad_backprop, output);
   }
*** End Patch

part.text:
Applied the TensorFlow `MaxPool3DGradGrad` safety checks to `tensorflow/core/kernels/pooling_ops_3d.cc` and validated the hunk against the target base.

```yaml
backport_result:
  status: success
  patch: |
    diff --git a/tensorflow/core/kernels/pooling_ops_3d.cc b/tensorflow/core/kernels/pooling_ops_3d.cc
    index 7345ccf69e..3192c0d640 100644
    --- a/tensorflow/core/kernels/pooling_ops_3d.cc
    +++ b/tensorflow/core/kernels/pooling_ops_3d.cc
    @@ -697,11 +697,24 @@ class MaxPooling3dGradGradOp : public OpKernel {
     
         Pool3dParameters params{context,  ksize_,       stride_,
                                 padding_, data_format_, tensor_in.shape()};
    +    if (!context->status().ok()) return;  // params is invalid
     
         Tensor* output = nullptr;
         OP_REQUIRES_OK(context, context->forward_input_or_allocate_output(
                                     {2}, 0, tensor_out.shape(), &output));
     
    +    OP_REQUIRES(context,
    +                tensor_in.NumElements() == out_grad_backprop.NumElements(),
    +                errors::InvalidArgument("tensor_in and out_grad_backprop must "
    +                                        "have same number of elements, got <",
    +                                        tensor_in.DebugString(), "> and <",
    +                                        out_grad_backprop.DebugString(), ">"));
    +    OP_REQUIRES(
    +        context, tensor_out.NumElements() == output->NumElements(),
    +        errors::InvalidArgument(
    +            "tensor_out and output must have same number of elements, got <",
    +            tensor_out.DebugString(), "> and <", output->DebugString(), ">"));
    +
         LaunchMaxPooling3dGradGradOp<Device, T>::launch(
             context, params, tensor_in, tensor_out, out_grad_backprop, output);
       }
  notes: >
    Backported the invalid Pool3dParameters early return and element-count validation checks for MaxPool3DGradGrad.
```
